title: Secret handling, masking, and leakage paths
description: Secrets are masked in logs but leak via echo to files, env dumps, error output of forked code, and being absent from fork PRs.
body: Use when a workflow consumes a secret. GitHub masks known secret values in logs, but masking does not stop leakage when a secret is written to an artifact, printed transformed (base64), inherited by a child process, or exposed to attacker code in a pull_request_target job. Secrets are not provided to workflows triggered by fork PRs under pull_request. Do not pass secrets into steps that run untrusted code or persist them to artifacts/caches. Actions: reference secrets only in trusted jobs, avoid echoing or transforming them, and prefer OIDC short-lived creds over stored secrets. Validation signals include secrets absent from artifacts, logs, and fork-triggered jobs. Risk boundary: any path that writes a secret to an artifact or exposes it to fork code is a blocking finding.
